# Agentic Coding Workflow

> Operational hub for the DAGent feature-branch pipeline.
>
> Architecture lives in
> [`tools/autonomous-factory/docs/architecture.md`](../tools/autonomous-factory/docs/architecture.md).
> This file is the operator runbook: configuration, commands, environment.

## Overview

The pipeline is a **Temporal-backed, DAG-shaped feature-branch automation**.
A worker process (`dagent-worker`) executes a deterministic workflow that
walks a feature spec from kickoff to PR; activities invoke specialist
agents via the GitHub Copilot SDK, run shell scripts, poll CI, and
classify failures. Operators talk to a running pipeline through the
`dagent-admin` Temporal client (signals, queries, updates).

**Key design principles:**

- **Deterministic orchestration** — Pipeline logic is a Temporal workflow.
  State transitions are code, not prompts. Workflow code is
  replay-deterministic; ESLint enforces the constraints.
- **DAG scheduling** — Each app declares its workflow in
  `<app>/.apm/workflows.yml`. Independent nodes run in parallel; failures
  trigger declarative on-failure routes through the triage classifier.
- **Linear branch model** — All work happens on a single
  `feature/<slug>` branch. The PR to the base branch (default `main`,
  override with `--base-branch` or `BASE_BRANCH=…`) is the final
  administrative step.
- **SDK sessions** — Each agent activity opens its own Copilot SDK
  session with a tailored system prompt assembled from the APM manifest.
  Stateless and disposable; tool-call limits are enforced by the harness.
- **Structural intelligence** — `roam-code` v11.2 (Python AST semantic
  graph, MCP-served) provides agents with codebase exploration. The
  index is built before any agent runs.
- **Single source of state** — The Temporal workflow event history is
  authoritative. Per-feature artefacts under `<app>/.dagent/<slug>/` are
  advisory: agent inputs/outputs, JSONL telemetry, screenshots.

---

## Project Structure

Items marked `(auto)` are generated by tooling; `(manual)` are created by
the team; `(git-ignored)` are not committed.

```
<repo-root>/
│
├── package.json                                    (manual)  Workspace root — pipeline scripts
├── .nvmrc                                          (manual)  Node 22
│
├── .devcontainer/
│   └── devcontainer.json                           (manual)  --shm-size=2gb, --ipc=host required
│
├── .github/
│   ├── AGENTIC-WORKFLOW.md                         (manual)  THIS FILE — operational hub
│   ├── copilot-instructions.md                     (manual)  Routing rules (always injected into Copilot)
│   └── workflows/
│       ├── agentic-feature.yml                     (manual)  Run the agentic pipeline in CI
│       ├── ci-integration.yml                      (manual)  Unit tests + builds (storefront)
│       ├── deploy-storefront.yml                   (manual)  PWA Kit deploy to Managed Runtime
│       └── temporal-it.yml                         (manual)  Temporal integration tests
│
├── infra/temporal/
│   └── docker-compose.yml                          (manual)  Postgres-backed Temporal cluster
│
├── tools/autonomous-factory/                       (manual)  Engine — Temporal workflow + activities
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── workflow/                                         Deterministic pipeline workflow + DagState
│   │   ├── activities/                                       Side-effecting work (agent / shell / CI / triage / archive)
│   │   ├── activities/support/                               Pure helpers shared by activities
│   │   ├── worker/                                           Worker bootstrap (registers workflows + activities)
│   │   ├── client/                                           dagent-admin + run-feature CLI
│   │   ├── domain/                                           Pure DAG math + transitions + scheduling
│   │   ├── ports/                                            Hexagonal port interfaces
│   │   ├── adapters/                                         I/O concretions (git, gh, shell, Copilot SDK, …)
│   │   ├── apm/                                              APM compiler + context loader + agent factory
│   │   ├── triage/                                           Failure classifier + handoff builder
│   │   ├── lifecycle/                                        Preflight checks + hooks + state-commit mutex
│   │   ├── harness/                                          Tool RBAC + circuit breaker + report_outcome
│   │   ├── telemetry/                                        JSONL pipeline-logger + console rendering
│   │   ├── session/                                          Activity-side helpers (git snapshots, SDK events)
│   │   ├── entry/                                            Composition root for client-side bootstrap
│   │   ├── contracts/                                        Cross-cutting NodeContext / NodeResult shape
│   │   └── paths/                                            feature-paths.ts — slug → on-disk paths
│   ├── docs/
│   │   ├── architecture.md                                   Topology, state, admin verbs, versioning
│   │   ├── 02-roam-code.md                                   Structural code intelligence
│   │   └── adr/0001-temporal.md                              Temporal migration decision + determinism
│   ├── agent-commit.sh                                       Scoped git commit wrapper
│   ├── agent-branch.sh                                       Feature branch lifecycle wrapper
│   ├── poll-ci.sh                                            CI workflow polling utility
│   ├── setup-roam.sh                                         Roam-code installation bootstrap
│   └── package.json                                          orchestrator workspace
│
└── apps/commerce-storefront/                       (manual)  Reference app (PWA Kit)
    ├── .apm/                                                 APM context configuration
    │   ├── apm.yml                                 (manual)  Master manifest (single source of truth)
    │   ├── workflows.yml                           (manual)  DAG definition + on-failure routes
    │   ├── instructions/                           (manual)  Per-role rule fragments (.md)
    │   ├── mcp/                                    (manual)  MCP server declarations
    │   ├── skills/                                 (manual)  Skill definitions
    │   ├── hooks/                                  (manual)  Lifecycle shell hooks
    │   └── .compiled/context.json                  (auto)    Compiled APM output
    │
    └── .dagent/<slug>/                             (auto)    Per-feature workspace
        ├── _kickoff/spec.md                        (auto)    Staged by stage-spec node
        ├── _LOG.jsonl                              (auto)    Telemetry stream
        └── <node>/<inv-id>/inputs|outputs|logs/    (auto)    Per-invocation artefacts
```

## Pipeline Commands

The orchestrator is two binaries, both exposed as npm scripts:

| Binary | Source | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `dagent-worker` | [`src/worker/main.ts`](../tools/autonomous-factory/src/worker/main.ts) | Long-running Temporal worker that polls the task queue and executes activities |
| `dagent-admin` | [`src/client/admin.ts`](../tools/autonomous-factory/src/client/admin.ts) | Temporal client — start, signal, query, update, cancel a workflow |

Plus the feature-start CLI:

| Script | Source | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `npm run agent:run` | [`src/client/run-feature.ts`](../tools/autonomous-factory/src/client/run-feature.ts) | Compile APM context → start workflow execution |

`dagent-admin` verb reference (verbatim from `--help`):

```
Verbs:
  Signals:
    cancel    <slug> [--reason <s>]                 — terminal halt with reason

  Updates (admin mutate-and-return):
    reset-scripts <slug> --category <c> [--max-cycles N]
    resume-after-elevated <slug> [--max-cycles N]
    recover-elevated <slug> --error <msg> [--max-fail-count N] [--max-dev-cycles N]

  Queries:
    status    <slug>                                — full StateSnapshot (JSON)
    progress  <slug>                                — count summary + percent
    next      <slug>                                — ready-to-dispatch batch
    summary   <slug>                                — terminal summary snapshot

Common options:
  --workflow <name>      Workflow name (default: storefront)
  --reason <text>        Human reason (cancel only)
  --category <c>         Script-node category (reset-scripts only)
  --error <msg>          Elevated-apply error message (recover-elevated only)
  --max-cycles N         Cycle budget override
  --max-fail-count N     Fail-count budget override
  --max-dev-cycles N     Dev-cycle budget override

Environment:
  TEMPORAL_ADDRESS    Temporal frontend gRPC address (default localhost:7233)
  TEMPORAL_NAMESPACE  Namespace (default default)

Exit codes:
  0  success
  1  invocation error or workflow not found
  2  update succeeded but reducer reports halted=true (cycle budget exhausted)
```

There is **no shell-level state-mutating verb** (`complete`, `fail`,
`reset-for-dev`, etc.). State mutations are owned by the workflow.
Activities surface `NodeResult` payloads; admin updates encapsulate
the few corrective actions an operator can take.

The `nuke` verb tears down a stuck run end-to-end (terminate workflow +
optionally delete branch + flush per-feature workspace):

```bash
npm run admin -- nuke <slug> --confirm [--delete-branch] [--app apps/commerce-storefront]
# Without --confirm, prints the plan and exits non-zero (safe dry-run).
```

---

## Git Operations

All agents use wrapper scripts — raw `git add/commit/push` is prohibited.

### `tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh`

Scoped commit. Stages only the paths declared for the named scope, plus
`.dagent/` so per-feature artefacts always land with the work.

| Scope | Default Staged Paths (relative to `APP_ROOT`) |
|---|---|
| `frontend` | `overrides/`, `app/`, `pages/`, `e2e/`, `.dagent/` |
| `e2e` | `e2e/`, `.dagent/` |
| `docs` | `README.md`, `docs/`, `archive/`, `.dagent/`, `.github/` |
| `pipeline` | `.dagent/` |
| `pr` | `archive/`, `.dagent/`, `PR_BODY.md` |
| `all` | All directories |

Apps may override defaults via `commitScopes` in `.apm/apm.yml`. When the
manifest defines them, the orchestrator passes explicit paths as
arguments, overriding these defaults.

Usage: `bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh <scope> "<message>" [explicit-paths...]`

**Auto-staging.** `agent-commit.sh` automatically stages
`package-lock.json` whenever any `package.json` is in the staged
changeset. This prevents lockfile desync that causes `npm ci` failures.

**Pre-commit rebase.** Before staging, `agent-commit.sh` runs
`git pull --rebase origin <branch>` to minimise merge conflicts when
parallel agents commit to the same branch.

### `tools/autonomous-factory/agent-branch.sh`

| Command | Effect |
|---|---|
| `create-feature <slug>` | Stash → checkout `$BASE_BRANCH` → pull → create `feature/<slug>` |
| `push` | Validates not on main; checks commits ahead; retries `--force-with-lease` on diverged history |
| `cleanup` | Deletes local `feature/` branches |
| `revert` | **Clean-slate reset** — hard-resets the current feature branch to `origin/${BASE_BRANCH}`. Preserves per-feature pipeline state under `.dagent/` by stashing it before `git reset --hard` + `git clean -fd`, then restoring. Used when a dev agent is stuck in a hallucination loop. |

### `tools/autonomous-factory/poll-ci.sh`

| Exit Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `0` | All CI workflows passed |
| `1` | One or more workflows failed (or were manually cancelled) |
| `2` | Timeout (configurable via `POLL_MAX_RETRIES`, default 10 retries × 30s) |

The `agentic-feature.yml` CI workflow sets `POLL_MAX_RETRIES=60` for
~30 min polling. Default is 10 (~5 min) for manual use.

**Failure log capture.** On failure, the script fetches truncated runner
logs via `gh run view <RUN_ID> --log-failed | tail -n 250` and echoes
them to stdout so the triage activity can route the failure.

---

## Running the Orchestrator

The orchestrator is **app-agnostic**. The `--app` flag tells it which
app directory to operate on. Each app must provide:

- **`.apm/apm.yml`** — APM manifest declaring agents, instruction
  includes, MCP servers, skills, runtime config (URLs, directories,
  test commands), token budgets, security profile.
- **`.apm/workflows.yml`** — DAG definition: nodes, dependencies,
  on-failure routes, triage profiles.

```bash
# Required: --app, --workflow, --spec-file, plus positional <slug>
npm run agent:run -- \
  --app apps/commerce-storefront \
  --workflow storefront \
  --spec-file /tmp/spec.md \
  <slug>

# Optional: --base-branch <name>  (defaults to $BASE_BRANCH or main)
```

On startup the orchestrator validates:

1. The `--app` directory exists.
2. `<app>/.apm/apm.yml` exists and passes Zod schema validation.
3. `--workflow` resolves to a workflow declared in
   `<app>/.apm/workflows.yml`.
4. `--spec-file` exists on disk.

**Resuming an interrupted run** is the same command. The
`create-branch` and `stage-spec` DAG nodes at the head of every
workflow are idempotent. The Temporal workflow picks up from the
persisted history.

### Multi-feature concurrency

Multiple features run as separate workflow executions on the same
worker. The `dagent-worker` task queue defaults to `dagent-pipeline`;
override with `TEMPORAL_TASK_QUEUE`.

### Detached runs

For long runs that need to survive editor reloads, use
[`scripts/run-agent.sh`](../scripts/run-agent.sh). It wraps the same
CLI in `systemd-run --user --scope` with a `MemoryMax` cap, falling
back to `setsid nohup`.

---

## Temporal (local development)

A devcontainer rebuild is required when first picking up the engine —
the Temporal CLI install and the `ajv` postinstall shim must run.
Verify with:

```bash
temporal --version
```

### Start a local Temporal cluster

Two run modes — pick one.

```bash
# (a) Foreground dev server — recommended for ad-hoc work.
#     In-memory persistence; Ctrl-C discards everything.
temporal server start-dev --ui-port 8233
# Frontend at localhost:7233, Web UI at localhost:8233.

# (b) Docker-compose stack — matches CI; required for integration tests
#     that exercise durable history.
docker compose -f infra/temporal/docker-compose.yml up -d
```

The `npm run temporal:dev --workspace=orchestrator` script starts the
foreground dev server with the project's defaults.

### Build + run the worker

```bash
npm run worker --workspace=orchestrator
# = tsc -p tsconfig.json && node dist/worker/main.js
```

**Workers run from compiled JS, NOT `tsx`.** Webpack inside
`@temporalio/worker` (used to bundle workflow code) is incompatible
with `tsx`'s global module-resolution hook. The npm scripts handle the
build automatically.

### Smoke tests

```bash
npm run temporal:hello       --workspace=orchestrator   # hello workflow end-to-end
npm run temporal:skeleton    --workspace=orchestrator   # skeleton pipeline
npm run temporal:dispatch    --workspace=orchestrator   # single-activity dispatch
```

### Run integration tests

```bash
npm run temporal:test:integration --workspace=orchestrator
```

### Tear down

```bash
docker compose -f infra/temporal/docker-compose.yml down
# or Ctrl-C the foreground dev server
```

---

## CI/CD Integration

This repo ships four workflows under `.github/workflows/`:

| Workflow | Trigger | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `ci-integration.yml` | Push to `feature/**` | Unit tests + builds (storefront, orchestrator). Polled by `poll-ci.sh`; failures feed into the triage cascade. |
| `temporal-it.yml` | Push / PR | Temporal integration tests against a docker-compose Postgres cluster. |
| `agentic-feature.yml` | `workflow_dispatch` | Runs the full agentic pipeline in CI (120-min timeout). Inputs: `feature-slug`, `app-path`, `workflow-name`, `base-branch`, `spec-content`. |
| `deploy-storefront.yml` | Push to `main` / `feature/**` on `apps/commerce-storefront/**` | PWA Kit build + Salesforce Managed Runtime deploy. |

### Required secrets

| Secret | Used by | What it is |
|---|---|---|
| `COPILOT_PAT` | `agentic-feature.yml` | GitHub PAT with `copilot`, `repo`, `read:org` scopes — used by SDK agent sessions. |
| `MOBIFY_API_KEY` | `deploy-storefront.yml` | Salesforce Managed Runtime Admin API key. |

Trigger an agentic run from the CLI:

```bash
gh workflow run "Agentic Feature Pipeline" \
  -f feature-slug=my-feature \
  -f app-path=apps/commerce-storefront \
  -f workflow-name=storefront \
  -f base-branch=main \
  -f spec-content="$(cat /tmp/spec.md)"
```

---

## Spec File

The spec file is the **only manual input** the pipeline requires. It is
NOT auto-generated.

**Provided via:** `--spec-file <path>` CLI flag. The path can live
anywhere readable by the orchestrator process (e.g. `/tmp/spec.md`, an
issue export, a separate docs repo).

**Staged by the pipeline.** The first DAG node that touches the
filesystem is `stage-spec`, which copies `$SPEC_FILE` into
`<app>/.dagent/<slug>/_kickoff/spec.md`. Downstream agents consume it
via the input-materialisation step as `inputs/spec.md` in their
per-invocation directory — no agent reads the CLI-supplied path
directly.

### Storefront spec template

```markdown
# Feature: <name>

## Goal
<desired outcome in 1-2 sentences>

## User-visible behaviour
- [ ] <observable acceptance criterion>

## Out of scope
- <explicit non-goals>

## Test data hints (optional)
- Locale / site: <e.g. en-US, RefArchGlobal>
- Product hint: <e.g. a product with at least 3 swatches and stock>
```

The `spec-compiler` node turns the spec into a machine-checkable
`acceptance` artefact (`ACCEPTANCE.yml` shape) that the rest of the
pipeline consumes. Hints in the spec become resolution input for the
test-data layer; the spec-compiler is responsible for picking concrete
fixtures (URLs, product IDs) that satisfy the contract under the
running storefront config.

---

## Environment Setup

### DevContainer Requirements

The DevContainer must be configured for Playwright browser testing:

```jsonc
{
  "runArgs": [
    "--shm-size=2gb",   // Chromium crashes at Docker's 64 MB /dev/shm default
    "--ipc=host"        // Required for headless browser stability
  ]
}
```

**Required tooling** (the devcontainer provides all of these):

- **Node.js >=22** (from `.nvmrc`)
- **Python 3.11** (for the roam-code orchestrator toolchain)
- **GitHub CLI** (`gh`) — Copilot SDK auth + CI polling
- **Temporal CLI** — local dev cluster
- **Playwright Chromium** — installed via `npx playwright install chromium`

**Required auth** (after DevContainer startup):

```bash
# GitHub CLI — required for Copilot SDK auth and CI polling
gh auth login
```

Pre-flight runs the configured `hooks.preflightAuth` command if the app
declares one and warns early when CLI auth is missing. GitHub CLI auth
is checked implicitly when the Copilot SDK initialises.

---

## Safety Guardrails

| Rule | Enforcement |
|---|---|
| **Pipeline state owned by the workflow** | Activities return `NodeResult`; the workflow folds them into `DagState`. Operators mutate state through admin signals/updates only. |
| **Per-node retry budget** | `failItem` reducer halts the workflow after the configured retry ceiling per node (default 10). |
| **Per-feature redev cycle budget** | `resetForDev` halts after 5 post-deploy → dev reroute cycles. Override via `recover-elevated --max-dev-cycles`. |
| **Per-feature redeploy cycle budget** | `resetForRedeploy` halts after 3 cycles (configurable via `max_redeploy_cycles` in `workflows.yml`). |
| **Cognitive circuit breaker** | Per-agent tool-call limits declared in `.apm/apm.yml` (`toolLimits: { soft, hard }`). Soft injects a frustration prompt; hard force-disconnects the SDK session. Resolution: per-agent → `config.defaultToolLimits` (60/80) → code fallback (30/40). |
| **Workflow determinism** | ESLint enforces no `Date.now()`, `Math.random()`, `setTimeout`, `node:fs`, adapter/port imports, or LLM SDK imports inside `src/workflow/`. See [ADR 0001](../tools/autonomous-factory/docs/adr/0001-temporal.md). |
| **Workflow versioning** | `npm run lint:workflow-version` fails CI if `src/workflow/**` changed without a `WORKFLOW_VERSION` bump or `patched(<id>)` wrap. |
| **Build before run** | Workers run from `dist/`, not `tsx` — webpack-inside-Temporal is incompatible with `tsx`'s resolver hook. |
| **No raw git commands** | All agents must use `agent-commit.sh` and `agent-branch.sh`. |
| **No push to main** | `agent-branch.sh push` validates branch is not `main`. |
| **Clean-slate revert** | After ≥3 dev failures on the same item, the workflow injects a warning advising `agent-branch.sh revert`. The circuit breaker grants one bypass so the revert opportunity can fire. |
| **Manifest validation** | Startup runs Zod validation on `apm.yml` and `workflows.yml`. |
| **Pre-flight checks** | Junk files, .dagent artifacts, optional cloud CLI auth — all validated before the first agent session. |
| **Graceful shutdown** | Temporal cancellation propagates to in-flight activities; `dagent-admin cancel <slug> --reason` is the documented kill-switch. |
